settings.title: Fifteen Years of Keeping Local Books in Order
settings.excerpt: We are celebrating fifteen years by thanking the first client who is still with us today.
settings.body:
Ledgerworks opened fifteen years ago above a hardware shop on the high street. Our very first client was the shop downstairs, and remarkably they are still on our books today.

A lot of accounting has gone digital since then, and we have changed with it. What has stayed the same is that most of our clients still come from someone recommending us to a neighbour.

What fifteen years has looked like

The early days were paper ledgers, a filing cabinet, and a lot of walking down the stairs to ask the shop owner a question. Today the work runs through cloud software and bank feeds, but the shape of it has not really moved. We still sit across from local owners and help them understand where their business stands.

A few things stand out when we look back.

  • Our first client, the shop downstairs, is still with us
  • We moved from paper ledgers to cloud accounting along the way
  • Most new clients still arrive through a personal recommendation

Giving something back

To mark the anniversary we are sponsoring this year's local business awards. It feels right to give something back to the community that built this firm one introduction at a time.

Those awards celebrate exactly the kind of business we have spent fifteen years working alongside, the independent shops and small firms that keep the high street alive. We are proud to put our name behind them, and grateful to everyone who has trusted us with their books over the years.
